proc-root.name: Active Directory Compromise Response
proc-root.notes: End-to-end response for AD on-premise compromise including credential theft, lateral movement, ransomware staging, containment with preserved DC for recovery, and full AD forest recovery.

proc-detect.name: Detection
proc-detect.notes: Identify AD compromise indicators: anomalous LDAP queries, DCSync traffic, unusual service account behavior, Kerberoasting attempts.

proc-detect-dcsync.name: Detect DCSync Activity
proc-detect-dcsync.notes: Monitor for DRS replication requests from non-DC sources (Event ID 4662 with GUID for DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All).

proc-detect-kerberoast.name: Detect Kerberoasting
proc-detect-kerberoast.notes: Identify mass TGS requests for service accounts with weak encryption (RC4) from single source hosts.

proc-detect-anomalous-logon.name: Detect Anomalous Privileged Logons
proc-detect-anomalous-logon.notes: Alert on domain admin logons to non-Tier-0 systems, pass-the-hash indicators, and NTLM downgrade attacks.

proc-analysis.name: Analysis & Scoping
proc-analysis.notes: Determine extent of AD compromise: enumerate compromised accounts, identify persistence mechanisms (Golden/Silver tickets, AdminSDHolder, DCShadow), map lateral movement paths.

proc-enum-compromised.name: Enumerate Compromised Accounts
proc-enum-compromised.notes: Use AD logs and forensic analysis to identify all compromised user, service, and computer accounts. Check for LSASS dumps, Mimikatz artifacts, SAM database extraction.

proc-persistence-analysis.name: Identify AD Persistence Mechanisms
proc-persistence-analysis.notes: Check for Golden Ticket/Silver Ticket creation, AdminSDHolder modifications, DCShadow indicators, SID History injection, GPO-based persistence, and skeleton key deployment.

proc-lateral-path.name: Map Lateral Movement Paths
proc-lateral-path.notes: Use BloodHound data or equivalent to identify attack paths exploited. Determine which systems attacker accessed via RDP, WinRM, SMB, PsExec.

proc-contain.name: Containment
proc-contain.notes: Contain the incident by isolating compromised systems while preserving one clean DC for recovery. Block lateral movement, disable compromised accounts, restrict SMB/RPC. Contain one DC by network isolation to use as recovery source.

proc-isolate-dc-recovery.name: Isolate Clean DC for Recovery
proc-isolate-dc-recovery.notes: Identify the least-compromised DC, network-isolate it completely (no inbound/outbound except management VLAN). This preserved DC will serve as the recovery seed for the AD forest. Verify its NTDS.dit integrity before relying on it.

proc-disable-accounts.name: Disable Compromised Accounts
proc-disable-accounts.notes: Disable all identified compromised accounts including domain admins, service accounts, and computer accounts. Reset passwords for tier-0 accounts immediately.

proc-block-lateral.name: Block Lateral Movement
proc-block-lateral.notes: Apply emergency firewall rules blocking SMB (445), RPC (135), WinRM (5985/5986), RDP (3389) between workstation segments. Allow only DC-to-DC replication on isolated recovery DC.

proc-disable-trusts.name: Disable Forest/Domain Trusts
proc-disable-trusts.notes: Temporarily disable all inter-forest and external domain trusts to prevent trust-based lateral movement and credential pivoting.

proc-ransomware-check.name: Ransomware Activity Assessment
proc-ransomware-check.notes: Assess whether AD compromise has been used to stage or deploy ransomware via GPO, PsExec, or scheduled tasks. Check for encryption staging, shadow copy deletion, and ransomware payload distribution.

proc-check-gpo-ransomware.name: Check GPO for Ransomware Deployment
proc-check-gpo-ransomware.notes: Audit all GPOs for unauthorized startup scripts, scheduled tasks, or software installation policies that could deploy ransomware payloads domain-wide.

proc-check-shadow-copies.name: Check for Shadow Copy Deletion
proc-check-shadow-copies.notes: Verify VSS shadow copies on servers and endpoints. Alert on vssadmin delete, wmic shadowcopy delete, or bcdedit recovery disable commands in event logs.

proc-check-staging.name: Check for Ransomware Staging
proc-check-staging.notes: Search SYSVOL, NETLOGON, and common admin shares for staged ransomware binaries. Check for PsExec/WMIC execution patterns distributing payloads.

proc-eradicate.name: Eradication
proc-eradicate.notes: Remove all attacker persistence from AD: reset KRBTGT twice, remove unauthorized GPOs, clean AdminSDHolder, remove rogue SPNs, purge DCShadow artifacts.

proc-krbtgt-reset.name: Reset KRBTGT Account (Double Reset)
proc-krbtgt-reset.notes: Reset KRBTGT password twice with sufficient replication interval (10+ hours between resets) to invalidate all Golden Tickets. Coordinate timing to minimize authentication disruption.

proc-clean-gpos.name: Remove Malicious GPOs
proc-clean-gpos.notes: Remove all unauthorized or modified GPOs. Restore GPOs from verified backup. Audit SYSVOL for unauthorized scripts or binaries.

proc-remove-persistence.name: Remove AD Persistence Mechanisms
proc-remove-persistence.notes: Clean AdminSDHolder ACL modifications, remove SID History injections, delete rogue computer/service accounts, remove unauthorized DACL/ACE entries on sensitive objects (Domain NC head, AdminSDHolder, Schema).

proc-reset-all-passwords.name: Forest-Wide Password Reset
proc-reset-all-passwords.notes: Reset passwords for ALL domain accounts in phased approach: Tier-0 (DAs, EAs, Schema Admins) first, then service accounts, then standard users. Reset all computer account passwords via scheduled netdom resetpwd.

proc-recover.name: Recovery
proc-recover.notes: Rebuild AD forest from contained clean DC. Restore DCs from verified backups or rebuild from scratch using contained DC as authoritative source. Re-establish replication, re-enable trusts, validate authentication services.

proc-rebuild-dcs.name: Rebuild Domain Controllers
proc-rebuild-dcs.notes: Using the isolated clean DC as seed, rebuild additional DCs from clean OS media. Promote them using IFM from verified NTDS.dit or allow standard replication from the recovered DC. Do NOT restore DCs from pre-incident backups if KRBTGT compromise is confirmed.

proc-validate-replication.name: Validate AD Replication
proc-validate-replication.notes: Run repadmin /replsummary, dcdiag /v, and verify all naming contexts replicate cleanly. Confirm SYSVOL replication via DFS-R is healthy.

proc-restore-services.name: Restore Dependent Services
proc-restore-services.notes: Progressively restore services dependent on AD: DNS, DHCP, Certificate Services, Exchange/M365 hybrid, application SSO. Validate Kerberos authentication end-to-end.

proc-lessons.name: Lessons Learned
proc-lessons.notes: Post-incident review: assess Tier model implementation gaps, credential hygiene failures, missing detections for DCSync/Kerberoasting, backup strategy for AD, and need for PAW/ESAE architecture.

ref-003.name: ANSSI Active Directory Security Hardening Guide

bo-001.scope: All identity and authentication services within the AD forest
bo-001.purpose: Ensure trusted authentication and authorization for all organizational resources
bo-001.context: AD compromise undermines all trust in the environment as the identity provider is the root of trust for on-premise resources

bo-002.scope: All AD-dependent business applications and services
bo-002.purpose: Maintain operational continuity of AD-authenticated services during incident response
bo-002.context: AD outage during recovery impacts every domain-joined system and Kerberos-authenticated application

bo-003.scope: AD forest recovery capability and domain controller infrastructure
bo-003.purpose: Guarantee ability to restore AD to a trusted state within defined RTO
bo-003.context: Full AD forest recovery is one of the most complex disaster recovery scenarios; preparation is critical

smg-001.context: Credential theft is the primary vector for AD compromise escalation
smg-001.scope: All privileged accounts (Tier-0, Tier-1) and service accounts
smg-001.purpose: Measure credential exposure and rotation compliance
smg-001.focus: Time since last password change for privileged accounts, detection of credential dumping
smg-001.criteria: All Tier-0 accounts rotated within 24h of incident detection; no stale privileged accounts

smg-002.context: Recovery speed determines business impact duration
smg-002.scope: Domain controller infrastructure and AD replication topology
smg-002.purpose: Measure AD forest recovery readiness and execution speed
smg-002.focus: Time to restore functional AD authentication from contained DC seed
smg-002.criteria: First DC operational within 4 hours; full replication restored within 24 hours

sm-001.description: Percentage of Tier-0 accounts with credentials rotated within SLA after compromise detection
sm-001.goal: Ensure compromised credentials cannot be reused for re-entry after containment
sm-001.method: Track password last-set timestamp against incident detection time for all Tier-0 accounts
sm-001.interpretation: 100% indicates all privileged credentials rotated; below 100% indicates residual compromise risk
sm-001.reporting: Hourly during active incident; daily during recovery phase

sm-002.description: Time elapsed from incident declaration to functional AD authentication restoration
sm-002.goal: Minimize business disruption by achieving rapid AD recovery
sm-002.method: Measure elapsed time from containment completion to successful Kerberos authentication validation
sm-002.interpretation: Under 4 hours is excellent; 4-12 hours is acceptable; over 24 hours indicates DR procedure gaps
sm-002.reporting: Post-incident report; quarterly DR drill results

bm-001.description: Count of Tier-0 accounts with password reset after incident detection timestamp

bm-002.description: Total count of Tier-0 accounts in the forest

bm-003.description: Elapsed hours from containment completion to validated AD authentication restoration
